source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A submerged scuba diver hears the sound of a boat horn directly above her on the surface of the lake. At the same time, a friend on dry land also hears the horn (\textbf{figure}). The horn is \( 1.2\,\text{m} \) above the surface of the water. Both air and water are at \( 20^{\circ}\text{C} \). What is the distance (labeled “?”) from the horn to the diver?

image_dependency: question explicitly references a figure and asks for the distance labeled '?'
the text mentions a friend on dry land hearing the horn at the same time, but no land distance or geometry is stated in text, suggesting essential quantitative information is in the image
registry_passed=true with one available image asset
multi_step: solving likely requires combining information from the figure with physics facts about sound in air and water at 20°C
the setup involves equal-hearing-time reasoning between two paths plus solving for an unknown distance
this is more than direct lookup because it requires interpreting geometry and relating distances through speed-time relationships
verifiability: answer is a specific numeric value ('90.8m'), which is easy to check against a derived result
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward answer validation
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and answer_type=short_text support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are well supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image dependency is acceptable here because the required figure asset exists
high verifiability and a specific short-text numeric answer make downstream checking reliable
multi-step physics reasoning value is reasonably strong, supporting inclusion in the candidate pool
